_comment: Wiring Map / feed-source registry for the Mission Control Feed layer. Each source describes WHERE a feed of Mission Packets comes from and HOW honest it is. wiring: live | partial | manual | placeholder | not wired. sourceType: agent-report | pr-feed | branch-feed | ci-feed | deploy-feed | security-feed | release-feed | repo-health | worker-feed | manual-status | placeholder. 'live' is ONLY allowed when a real integration genuinely exists and is verified — no source here is 'live' yet. 'needsNext' states the exact backend/API/webhook required to upgrade the wiring. 'lastVerified' is a date or the honest word 'never'.

sources.label: Manual Mission Packets
sources.summary: Hand-authored packets the owner/agents write directly. Honest, human-edited — the temporary source of truth until real feeds are wired.
sources.needsNext: Nothing — manual by design. Migrate into the operations DB (D1) later if/when that infra lane is approved.

sources.label: Agent Reports (from v1 registry)
sources.summary: Normalizes Mission Control v1's agent census into agent-report packets. Reported status only — Mission Control cannot see or control external Claude/Codex/Fly sessions.
sources.needsNext: A real agent check-in path (each agent POSTs a signed report to a Worker, or drops a JSON file into the Agent Feed Inbox). Until then this is a reported logbook, not telemetry.

sources.label: Agent Feed Inbox
sources.summary: A real place for agents to post reports: drop a JSON report into docs/agent-feed/inbox/ and add a line to feed/data/inbox.json. Partial — the drop+index path is wired and read here, but ingestion is still manual (no Worker auto-collects reports).
sources.needsNext: A Worker (or scheduled job) that validates and indexes inbox reports automatically, plus an authenticated post endpoint so agents don't hand-edit the index.

sources.label: Owner Action Queue (from v1)
sources.summary: Normalizes the v1 owner-action queue into owner-action packets so 'what needs you now' surfaces in the feed too.
sources.needsNext: Nothing — manual by design. Could derive from real feeds once PR/CI/deploy feeds are live.

sources.label: PR / Branch Feed
sources.summary: PR feed NOT wired yet. The cards shown are normalized from the v1 reported PR board — they are NOT live from GitHub and must never be treated as verified CI/merge state.
sources.needsNext: Server-side ONLY GitHub adapter: a Cloudflare Worker holding the GitHub token as a secret (never in browser), calling the REST/GraphQL API or receiving webhooks, caching + rate-limiting responses, separating preview vs production branches, and writing branch/PR#/CI/review/conflict/deploy state + a last-verified timestamp into a feed the page can read. No token may ever be wired client-side.

sources.label: Build / CI Feed
sources.summary: Build/CI feed is a placeholder. The repo has one real GitHub Actions gate (coverage on leaderboard/shared/), but it is not yet read into Mission Control.
sources.needsNext: Same server-side Worker as the PR feed: read GitHub Actions run status (or receive check_run webhooks) and expose verified CI state. Never mark a build green unless the exact check completed successfully from a real source.

sources.label: Deploy Feed
sources.summary: Cloudflare Pages / Vercel deploy state is a placeholder. Deploy status today is reported manually in the owner action queue, not read from a deploy API.
sources.needsNext: A server-side adapter (Cloudflare Pages deployments API and/or Vercel deployments API) behind a Worker, with preview vs production clearly separated and a last-verified timestamp.

sources.label: Security / Dependency Feed
sources.summary: Security / dependency alerts are a placeholder. No Dependabot / advisory feed is wired into Mission Control yet.
sources.needsNext: A server-side adapter reading GitHub Dependabot / security advisories (Worker holds the token), surfaced as security-feed packets with severity. No secrets client-side.

sources.label: Worker / Health Feed
sources.summary: Health of deployed Workers (e.g. leaderboard /api/health, growverse-api /health) is a placeholder. Health is reported manually today, not polled.
sources.needsNext: A scheduled Worker that pings each service's /health endpoint and records the result + timestamp. Read-only pings only; no secrets.
